# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Docker image tag (e.g., v1.0, latest)')
        string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: 'myregistry.example.com/myorg/myrepo', description: 'Full URL to the Docker registry and repository (e.g., myregistry.example.com/myorg/myrepo)')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Build Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${params.REGISTRY_URL}:${params.IMAGE_TAG}"
                    echo "Building Docker image: ${fullImageName}"
                    sh "docker build -t ${fullImageName} ."
                }
            }
        }

        stage('Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${params.REGISTRY_URL}:${params.IMAGE_TAG}"
                    echo "Pushing Docker image: ${fullImageName}"
                    // Assuming Docker credentials are pre-configured in the agent or via a 'withDockerRegistry' block
                    // Example with 'withDockerRegistry' if credentials are needed:
                    // withDockerRegistry(credentialsId: 'your-docker-credential-id', url: "https://${params.REGISTRY_URL.split('/')[0]}") {
                    //     sh "docker push ${fullImageName}"
                    // }
                    sh "docker push ${fullImageName}"
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
